I went to the cinema yesterday to see this, and was pretty much going in with negative mood, from the comments I read on the youtube and heard from people. I never again watch and read comments and trailers anymore. I loved the movie, it exactly got what I loved. The action, stunts, plot twists and to be honest, I kinda like this sequel. Also, I didnt find any annoying character here as in some movies there are. The blue light and feel of the base where Jags were was cool! What is more, I thought that I would really be upset to see super kids saving the day, but to be honest, the Amara was not annoying kid, she was genuinely likeable character. I liked the characters even though they didnt have enough developement, maybe Im shallow. I really wish this would got a good box office and we would see trilogy. Please, dont get distracted by bad comments here. If you liked the first you will like this too. Also, about the fast moving Jaegers, they felt heavy and big. I loved that they were more agile. Not all Jaegers were doing the flips. At last, do you know why I loved Pacific Rim? I have never seen anyone say this but, the thought of humans joining together, and creating bigger robot, which have looks like us, which just reflect our creativity, logic, nature and physic understanding. That our physic weakness goes away. Im not sure if I said this good but here are mine words. Thanks for reading and have a great day!

This is what happens when a novice director and a bunch of TV writers trying to make a sequel without actually understanding what makes the first movie so great. They’re like kids given opportunity to play with an excellent original material but fall short when they thought that people want to see big robots fighting monsters and leveling cities, with shiny CGI and loud sounds is the main theme. Del Toro’s version is a human theme story, with Raleigh Beckett losing his brother and try to live with the memory, Pentecost’s protective father instinct on a little girl he help raised as his own, the strain father and son relationship between Chuck and Herc Hansen, Mako’s struggle to get revenge for her families death and desire to prove her self to Pentecost. All this are human emotions and story with robots vs monsters as backdrop. It’s how Del Toro can make a movie of a woman having sex with a fish man, an appealing award winning movie.

you know what i mean though? its like i went from watching something dark and sinister, to watching yet another transformers film, even the jaegers, they moved rather realistically like they had real weight to them, now they are jumping around and being tossed about like physics suddenly no longer applies to them - i bet you no kids die in this film, i bet they all survive and they all live happily ever after.

You have a director that has never done a big budget movie before (and a writer for the Transformers cinematic universe) and John Boyega (first time producer) stupidly wanting faster jagers. At the end of Pacific Rim the Kaiju were already moving pretty fast and the Jagers were keeping up. How fast would these new Kaiju be now to merit stupidly faster Jagers? It breaks the immersion of a giant fight. Just take out all the buildings and all you see are robots fighting aliens not some grand battle against giant beasts. 

And you also have the studio thing of let's have pre teen pilots to appeal to kids. And let's have more of everything (Jagers and Kaiju) so we can milk the toy market. That is literally what Transformers is and this is what Pacific Rim has become of it and you can see it in the trailers. If you can't see that the you are in denial and I hope you have some humility and admit when you see how shit this in the theater.

I agree. Shame it was not Guillermo helming this flick. Its hard not to go bigger, louder, more when it comes to sequels. You can if you have a great storyline, but I think too many of the younger movie goers expect bigger, louder, faster and more for a sequel that if it isn't...its given the thumbs down. The director & producer have said they are going for a younger audience with this one for the bigger box office, so expect to be disappointed if you were an older fan of the original. We also have a fem empowerment aspect for the kids with one of the jaeger pilots being a teenage girl who is supposed to be a shithot hacker and built her own jaeger.

Bob Reno well I'm glad you like the new one, but I don't, the appeal of the original for me is that it wasn't like Transformers where you have Kung fu robots running around. Creatures the size of skyscrapers have a tremendous amount of mass and that does not move fast. Del Toro showed a balance of accuracy and entertainment (by speeding them enough). As I said, edit out the buildings to remove scale and this would look more like guys in space armor fighting aliens than battle between giants. 

FYI, do you want to know why it's set at daytime? It's to help the Jagers colors stick out so you can tell them apart, because they lack originality of the different Jagers in the original. If you look at the new ones it's the same frame with different armor. Say what you want about Cherno Alpha, hate it or love it you could not mistake for any other Jager. As for the new ones, there was more that a few times in the trailers I lost track and couldn't tell which Jager was which.

Bob Reno sorry, forgot to address the other part of your question, well the aliens were sending giant monsters, they were huge armored beast far larger than anything else on the planet. after searching for the said quote, it was Boyega, my bad... but it's worth reading that part as a whole, you can see everyone on board was rolling with the idea of much lighter feeling mechs, etc.

''“Technology has jumped ahead” says on-set supervisor Dave Merritt, while showing us the more light-weight suits donned by Uprising’s pilots. “We wanted to be able to be lighter weight and more agile. I think he also wanted to add much more of the martial arts kind of feel to everything.”

Boyega, who shares a producing credit, is particularly invested in this fluidity. The actor admits that he found the movements of the original Jaegers a little too cumbersome, and has brought fight co-ordinator Liang Yang over from Star Wars: Rogue One and The Force Awakens to bring the aforementioned “martial arts feel” to Uprising.

“They were like, "What did you feel about the first film," and I told them straight up the stuff that I didn't like and the stuff I wanted to see more of,” says Bodega. “*I wish the robots would hurry up, just hurry up.*” Boyega and DeKnight’s talks also resulted in more of a connection between what’s going on in the Conn-Pod and the outside, so action sequences won’t jarringly jump from CG to something that’s been shot in the studio. “We have a way of blending those two worlds together,” says Boyega, “and once I heard that, I was like, "Okay, cool, that's going to be really, really good.”

“The way that Steven and [Executive Producer] Cale Boyter have been talking about it is that they want to have Borne Identity fights but with giant robots,” says Production Designer Stefan Dechant. ”There'll just be a much faster movement in there”. One of these new, faster Jaegers, Saber Athena, has rockets but also little air breaks so that it can be flipped, then it can jump back and forth. “There's that level of speed and momentum and that slickness,” says Dechant.''

To those here who are saying "Oh my god this is just transformers but bigger, it's gonna be shit" or "looks like power rangers and shit. Gonna be a terrible movie", stop and think for a moment. Lore wise, it makes perfect sense that the jeagers would evolve to become much more agile and maneuverable. Most of the jeagers in the first movie aside from Gipsy after being repaired and Striker were clunky and slow. Jeagers were destroyed because most of the time, the kaiju were much faster (i.e. Otachi blitzing Gipsy and Crimson).

Now look at this from an inspiration point of view. I can guarantee that these jeagers are inspired by Evas, Gundams, and Jet Jagur from Godzilla. This and the first one arent movies made to be some adaptation of an American cartoon. Pacific rim is supposed to be an American interpretation of the kaiju/mecha anime genre. Just look at how the jeagers move. They aren't moving like humans like a transformer and it's not cheesy and glamorized like a megazord. It's fluid but still robot like. You can tell they are being controlled and that they are still machines. 

Bottom line, comparisons with transformers and shit shouldn't be made because they are nothing alike

One thing i noticed: alot of people whenever they see a giant robot/ mecha they instantly think to transformers or power rangers when there is many other sources of inspiration like gundam, evangelion, robotech(macross), and so much more. But the general population doesn't think of those they just jump it to the 2 "original giant robots"

That's because MS are standardized, if not mass produced military vehicles, Gundam are merely prototype MS with ace pilots, Eva units are living organisms and Macross' mechs are literally aircraft. Unlike Eva units, they're clearly inorganic, there's no sign of military co-ordination or weaponry a la MSG, and they lack the sleek design principles of most Macross mechs. The Baygers look and act like Bayformers in the trailer.

I think some people are calling this film Transformers/Power Rangers not because of the robots, but the style of the film. Compared to the original Pacific Rim this movie feels childish and Bayformers-like. It's clear enough the original was inspired by Japanese Kaiju and Mecha animes and movies instead of Western ones.

The movie looks good, i can clearly see the difference in direction though. Both directors and the franchise in general are inspired in mecha anime like Gundam and evangelion. But Del Toro has always had a proclivity to make fantastic things as realistic looking as possible. Like when using so many real puppets in pan's laberynth or Hellboy 2, or when making the jaegers so slow in the first one so you could feel their weight. This new director on the other hand is going all out anime. Making these mechs do crazy stunts. Neither of these styles is right or wrong, they are just different.

I understand that everyone is getting angry over the fact that the Jaegers are faster and sleeker in this version, but it actually kind of makes sense. 

Uprising takes place, in Del Toro's words when he was still working on the project, "A few years after the first one". A few years could range from anywhere from 5 years to 15 years. Let's say it takes place 5 years ahead of the first one just in case. This amount of time allows for multitudes of upgrades to be made to the Jaeger design, and of course, the countries would continue to make them (because they would either be 1. Skeptical of a secondary alien invasion or 2. Skeptical of other countries abusing this mechanical power as a militaristic strength). When so many engineers and scientists are focussing on one thing at any given time, a LOT usually gets done. This means that the research and development could have skyrocketed without the impending Kaiju invasion. This explains why the Jaegers could be so sleek and faster. 

I know everyone's getting pissed off about it, but I mean we're all really here to see massive robots v kaiju action here, so I still have hope for it (even if it's just a little bit).

The Mark-6 Jaegers were built during the ten-year period following the Kaiju War. While some believed the Kaiju were no longer a threat, others chose to be cautious and were motivated to resume construction of the Jaegers. The Mark-6 Jaegers reportedly represent "the pinnacle of human invention". Mark-6 Jaeger's use "state-of-the-art" hologram technology, and stand at 250' tall.

Mark-6 Jaegers were designed with "sleeker frames" and use "high octane burst replusors". Speed and velocity were increased, and the armor plating of the Jaegers utilized newer, lightweight reinforced steel armor material. The fighting strength of Mark-6 Jaegers utilize over 30,000 pounds of force, allowing them to move in ways that were not possible for their predecessors.

The Mark-6 Jaegers were built during the ten-year period following the Kaiju War. While some believed the Kaiju were no longer a threat, others chose to be cautious and were motivated to resume construction of the Jaegers. The Mark-6 Jaegers reportedly represent "the pinnacle of human invention". Mark-6 Jaeger's use "state-of-the-art" hologram technology, and stand at 250' tall.

Mark-6 Jaegers were designed with "sleeker frames" and use "high octane burst replusors". Speed and velocity were increased, and the armor plating of the Jaegers utilized newer, lightweight reinforced steel armor material. The fighting strength of Mark-6 Jaegers utilize over 30,000 pounds of force, allowing them to move in ways that were not possible for their predecessors.

Additional weaponry used by Mark-6 Jaegers includes blades designed with plasma, and the gravity sling, a weapon that "turns everything into a weapon". In the particular case of Gipsy Avenger, a Mark-6 Jaeger built in honor of the memory of Gipsy Danger, utilizes two nuclear vortex turbines that generates enough energy to sustain the city of Chicago for a year.

Alright I noticed that people are thinking Transformers when they see this. Let me explain.

The Purpose of the Pan Pacific Defense Core was not only defeat Kaiju threats but also use as a way to share their technology with other countries in creating better Jagers without leaking top secret schematics. The Mark V Striker Eureka was the fastest one ever built, and it was essential to make faster Jagers since Catagory 4 Kaiju were smarter. In order to do so the builders found ways to make them slimmer and taller along with recreating the Drift System to help with reaction time.

For reference I look through the Universal Century of Gundam. That spanned Through 14 years where the Earth Federation and Zeon kept sending more Advanced Machines to battle another just as Advanced Machines. Pacific Rim Still is more relatable to Japanese Mecha Anime than Transformers.

I loved the first one and the tone of this seems so different. It's just a trailer so maybe the final product will be different but it felt like robots were moving too fast and were to agile which were always one of the issues I had with the transformers movies. One of the things I loved about the first movie was that it felt like the Jaegers and Kaiju had real weight behind them. They were big massive tanks, not robot ninjas jumping around.

Sad how the 'hip' thing is 'OMG it's not Directed by Del Toro it's just Transformers'....it is almost like nobody actually saw the first film. It was absolutely a cut above the average Transformers-type of blockbuster, but it was far from some flawless masterpiece.

The character archetypes used in the film were very pure and simple, they were all drawn in pretty one-note fashion. They've talked about how they are going for more complexity in the characters in this film.

Charlie Hunnam lacks charisma as a lead, ask the King Arthur movie. John Boyega has a lot more on-screen charisma.

The first film had pacing issues, and a middle chunk(Before the battle of Hong Kong) that takes too long to get going. It needed to be leaned out in the editing room. The Director is shooting for 2 hours with this film.

The first film shrouded all of its action scenes in rain, water, and night time. Interesting aesthetic choice, but fight scenes in broad daylight allow the fan to appreciate them in all of their glory.

The Director has stated his influences included Ultraman and Godzilla, which were among influences for Del Toro. They built off the Story Bible Del Toro developed on the first film. The notion of a Kaiju-Jaeger Hybrid? The concept of someone betraying humanity? Those are both ideas that Del Toro mentioned back in the early development of the sequel.

This movie is cut from the same cloth as the first film and honestly feels like a natural extension and evolution of that world. Only we're going to get a more charismatic lead, more interesting characters, and a better paced film. It's going to surprise everyone by being an improvement over the first film.

I know that It doesn't look as good as the first one, and there's a lot of stuff that people can legitimately criticize... but honestly, I just want to see Kaiju and Mechs punch each other in the face.

I have some concerns

1. The movements of the jaegers- They just look too fast. I've been reading the comments and seeing people complain about the movements being to fast and people defending it saying "Its set further in the future so of course they'd be faster". The complaint about movement speed is stemming from the fact that because of how slow the movements were in the first movie it gave a feeling of weight and power to the jaegers, combined with the sounds it really felt like a 300 ton punching machine. Along with that I'd describe the fighting style of each movie as a heavy weight boxing/wrestling match for the first movie and ninja like fighting for uprising. I'm not entirely opposed to the fast movement decision if it was limited to 1/3 to 1/2 the jaegers, not most if not all judging by the trailer.

2.The jaeger design- This isn't as big of a problem but I'm not optimistic about most of the designs. Most of the jaegers seem to be going for the striker/typhoon slim design(though I'd say S/T did it better). It just seems to take away from the power of the jaegers making them seem like less of a giant machine and more(as people in the comments have already pointed out) seem more like transformers or power rangers. I do however like the look of(forgive me for not knowing his actual name) mace fist.

3. The characters- With the exception of John Boyegas character, the new characters look like they're going to be susceptible to the media trend of adding strong young/minority/female characters only to completely miss the mark and make them feel forced, lazily written and instead of being a strong character coming off as bitchy and unlikable(take Sarah Palmer from halo 4-5 as an example). 

I love the first movie and yes, i know this is just a trailer and I'm desperately hoping its good, but the fact that its not being directed by Guillermo Del Toro is the main thing giving me concerns about this movie. The man knew what made his movie great and without his mind behind this, it is very hard to put faith in this sequel.

It just seems so lifeless, bland and unexciting. Where are the money shots? In Pacific Rim and every Bay movie there are so many insane shots. I don't feel any of these elements here. We've been waiting five years for... that?

To those who don't get why they don't just bomb them; they did. It didn't work too well. They needed a more direct way of causing damage, like giant assed swords that can deliver the power of a bomb, without nuking a whole city. This was kind of mentioned in the first film, if you had actually bothered to pay attention.

A handsome man went into a hotel and asked to see the boss. When the boss came, the story began.

-The client: is room 39 empty?
-The boss: yes, sir.
-The client: can I book it?
-The boss: of course you can.
-The client: thank you.

Before going to the room, the client asked the boss to provide him with a black knife, a white thread 39 cm and an orange 73g.

The boss agreed though he was surprized at the weird things the client asked to have.

The client went into his room, he didn't ask for food or anything else.

Unfortunately for the boss, his room was next to room 39.

After midnight, the boss heard strange voices and noise in that client's room. Voices of wild animals and of utensils and dishes being thrown on the floor.

The boss didn't sleep that night. He kept thinking and wondering what might be the source of the noise.

In the morning, when the client handed the keys to the boss, the latter asked to see the room first.

He went to the room and found everything alright. Nothing unusual. He even found the thread, the black knife and the orange on the table.

The client paid the bill and gave the bellboys a very good tip and left the hotel smiling.

The boss was in a shock but he didn't reveal what he heard to the bellboys. In fact, he started to doubt himself.

After one year, the client showed up again. He asked to see the boss again. The boss was in a puzzle.

The client asked the same things: room 39, black knife, white thread 39cm and an orange 79g.

This time, the boss wanted to know the truth by all means possible. He spent a sleepless night, waiting for something to happen. After midnight, the same voices and noises started, this time louder and more indecipherable than the year before.

Again, before leaving, the client paid his bill and left a large tip on the table for the bellboys. The smile didn't leave his face.

The boss started searching for the meaning of everything the client asked to have. Why did he ask room 39? why the white thread? why the black knife??? In fact, the boss didn't arrive to any convincing answer to all these questions.

The boss now was eagerly waiting for the month of March, the month in which the client showed up. 

To his surprise, on the first day of March, the same client showed up. He asked the same questions. Wanted to book the same room, wanted to have the same things as before.

The boss again heard the same noises, this time more louder than before.

In the morning, when the client was leaving the hotel, the boss apologized politely to the client and asked to know the secret behind the noises in the room.

-''If I tell you the secret, do you promise to never reveal it to anyone else?''
-''I promise I will never let anyone know''.
-''Swear''
-''I swear I won't reveal your secret''
So finally, the client revealed his secret to the boss. 

Unfortunately, the boss was a sincere person. Until now he hasn't revealed his secret to anyone.

When he does, I will let you know... thank you for reading.

I am not appreciating the complete 180 in ary direction here. The first movie had bright and contrasting colors, sure, but it still felt gritty and heavy-toned. This is bright and colorful as hell, it seems comical almost. And the way they animate the Jaegers and Kaijus is different too. Its more fluid, more human, most importantly, its too fast. The way the first movie animated it was perfect. Jaegers were slow, hulking masses of towering metal, you could feel the weight of every step it took, every impact it made. The Kaijus were the same as well, flesh bobbed to and fro as they moved, giving the feeling that they truly are hulking masses of flesh and bone. Honestly, judging from the trailers, I might pass. I still want this movie to be successful though, maybe we can get a third one directed by Guillermo and its actually done right :/

This only cements my fears. They've taken away what made the fights awesome in the original: the weight and scale. When Jeagers moved in the original, they didn't run, jump, and flip all over the place, they had to build up to that, because they were giant mechas. The weight behind their attacks made them feel way more satisfying to watch. The scaling also helped make the both the Jeagers and Kaiju seem huge and intimidating, making everything and everyone around them seem tiny and insignificant in comparison. And I know there's people out there who make the excuse that "these are more advanced Jeagers.", yeah, but they're still giant mechas, they shouldn't be doing a lot of the stuff seen here. By removing all of this they're making it look like a Bayformers movie. The Megazord in Power Rangers had more weight and somewhat better scaling.

Remember Striker Eureka?  It was a Mark V Jaeger.  These Jaegers are Mark VI's.  Plus the movie takes place 10 years after Pacific Rim

There is no material we can create that can sustain loads like that, accelerating a 1000+ ton machine forward that fucking quickly would rupture any actuator or hydraulic line. The titanium or hardened steel plate that these massive actuators are connected too would simply buckle. 

The more metal you add for strength, the more weight you need to carry, which means you need more power, which means more weight, power, weight, etc. There is a critical point in which you can't add more power, because you can't add more weight. 

And on top of that this machine has to sustain hundreds of thousands of more "Tons" as it pushes that creature back.

So yes, it makes 0 sense and this is a movie.

Lol I see a lot of comments saying that this movie lacks realism just because the jaegers are lightweight and can do backflips. The thing is, the majority of action-packed movie-goers do not expect realism in movies, especially a movie that involves giant robots and monsters. So when the producers think about this majority, it simply means more profit. If they make the movie too realistic, I believe not many people are gonna watch it, thus less profit. For the first movie, I heard many people didn't like the night battle, which is why they make the sequel with daylight battle. 

Personally, I like the first one. And I know the sequel is gonna be good. Not great though. I don't mind if the jaegers manage to backflip, although I kinda agree that it defies physics. But who cares? As long as there are giant robots in action, I'm in. 

Also, I think that most people who condemn this movie are actually gonna watch it, probably to condemn it more, which is kinda stupid. Lol.

Guillermo added something to the first one. Something other robot franchises failed in. The first one had a sense of reality to it. It could make you believe that it is really happening. The slow paced movement of the jaegers that gives you perspective on their weight and height. The “no hope” feel of the movie and most of all, arguably the greatest ‘sacrifice everything for victory’ monologue since independence day. This looks like a straight cgi overkill. A predictable storyline. The playboy/wildchild son of the great war hero who doesn’t think he’s good enough to fill his dad’s shoes but ultimately does. meh I think I’ll pass on this one. Bring in Guillermo and Charlie for the third one maybe I’ll watch it.

I absolutely cannot wait to watch this movie, but I won’t lie, I also see where many people are b****ing about, and this is going to take a while...
Yes the Jaegers move a little fast for their size, I get that, yet who knows, it could’ve been accurate as f*** in that time, I mean (and only Japanese fans could get this) look at another mech series Evangelion (one that some say these jaegers are copying) and they’re fast as f*** despite being taller than the buildings around, and that takes place in freaking 2015 (I suggest looking it up to see where I’m going), plus the Kaiju still maintain that slow build so I don’t see the problem there, also for English people, Striker Eureka was also quick to move, I mean seriously
Yes the battles taking in broad daylight may remove the seriousness the previous had, but just because it’s a battle to decide the fate of the planet doesn’t always be dark and brooding, though yeah, I get it makes the movie less fun, yet there’s still some dark moments this movie has
And I get where people are getting with everything else, like this movie looking like transformers, Im a bit iffy about the music as well and that last scene, was there a point in putting that in? Still, I suggest refraining from calling this s*** Before actually watching it

Looks like a bit of fun but doesn't have the same feel the first gave me to be honest. And it kind of feels like it undermines one of the major themes of the first film. While still being a Saturday morning cartoon of a film the first film still had a major theme of human beings uniting for once and being able to beat back a seemingly invincible foe because of it. But I may be overthinking a concept about robots punching monsters. 

Also a side note, the mechs don't look like they have any weight to them, at all. 

But i guess we'll see how good it is when it comes out.

I wasn’t too sure about this after I watched the first time, but after watching again I think they have some pretty cool ideas. In Voltron and Power Rangers the mechs combine into one giant robot to fight the bad guys, but it looks like they turned that on its head and are making the kaiju “morph” together into one bigger enemy. The white mechs that open the breach have 3 fingers, it appears, and look very alien. Are they somehow controlled by the kaiju masters? Who on earth would strike a deal with them, and why? I like that the jaegers are still very different from each other, as in the first film it was stated that the kaiju were “adapting” to human jaeger tactics. The young pilots seem odd at first, but I would have made a way better pilot at 17 than at 30, so maybe it’s not that weird. The kids in Ender’s Game (the book) accomplished feats adults couldn’t because their minds hadn’t been put into boxes yet. I buy that.

Honestly this trailer doesn’t look great, but that’s what I thought when I saw the trailer for the first film, as well. I only gave it a chance because Del Toro’s name was on it. I strapped myself in for a terrible action movie. But after that first fight, where Beckett has to watch his brother get ripped out of the cockpit, then gets his jaeger torn apart, I realized I was actually watching a good movie, and changed my attitude. It was cheesy, silly, full of one-liners, fake action and plot holes, but it was a GOOD MOVIE.

I've read through the comment section, and agree with a lot of you. I'll add my 2 cents though. What was awesome about the fights in the 1st movie was that you got the emotional impact of the fight, you felt some level of connection with each Yaeger's pilots, and when each one went down swinging, it brought a tear to my eye. The orchestral score was rhythmic and mechanical to match the mechanical nature of the Yaeger's. Now we have big Power Rangers basically. They fly, they transform, they fight in packs, they all have bright colors so you can tell which is which. Add in the teenager lead roles, and it's further proof this was a sequel that no one expected, and few people now want. If you want a great "kids save the day" film, watch Ender's Game!

I feel like this movie leans towards its “teenage audience” unlike the first film. You get those vibes just listening to this pop music, and I’d much prefer the dramatic orchestrated music. Also, I don’t complain about the physics behind the robots, but I feel their designs need more differentiation. The first film had robots that played unique roles; The tank, the mobile, the advanced and the underdog. Here, of course those roles are fulfilled but their general designs obviously look very similar. Furthermore, for a movie showing the human race once again on the edge of extinction, why in broad daylight? I’d much prefer seeing this mostly in a dark time scenario since it’d much better suit the theme of the monster apocalypse.
